purpose: Section-specific generate prompt for authoring the framework_mappings field (cross-framework anchor object keyed by framework). Composed at runtime with system_prompts/generate.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE AUTHORING: framework_mappings

framework_mappings names which AWS / Azure / GCP / NIST / OWASP / AIGP best practices the principle concretises or cross-references. The structure is an object keyed by framework identifier; each framework key holds a `references` array of one or more reference objects.

PRIMARY ANCHOR vs CROSS-REFERENCE

AWS is the catalogue's primary anchor — every principle MUST concretise an AWS implementation step (or explicitly extend with mapping_state: 'na'). AIGP and other frameworks (Azure, GCP, NIST, ISO 42001, EU AI Act) are cross-references — informational, no enforcement obligation.

ANCHORING METHODOLOGY

Principles anchor at the AWS implementation-step level by default. The framework_mappings.aws.references[0].implementation_step field carries { number, title, verbatim_text } pointing at the specific step the principle concretises.

COMMON REFERENCE FIELDS (every reference, every framework)

- mapping_state: one of 'verified' (confirmed against source documentation), 'unverified' (provisional), 'na' (no analogue in this framework).
- last_checked: YYYY-MM-DD when the reference was last assessed.
- note: free-text rationale — what our principle adds, why provisional, why no analogue.

FRAMEWORK-SPECIFIC REFERENCE FIELDS (aws)

lens, pillar, focus_area, question, best_practice, implementation_step { number, title, verbatim_text }.

FRAMEWORK-SPECIFIC REFERENCE FIELDS (aigp)

domain, competency.

DIMENSIONS YOUR DRAFT WILL BE SCORED ON

1. aws_anchor_present — framework_mappings.aws.references[0] exists with all required structural fields populated for verified state.
2. common_fields_complete — every reference has mapping_state + last_checked + note.
3. verbatim_text_matches_source — implementation_step.verbatim_text is the AWS step text exactly.
4. mapping_state_honest — verified used only for references checked against source; unverified for provisional; na only when no analogue exists.
5. note_explains_value_add — the AWS note explains what our principle adds beyond the AWS step (where it lives, who validates, when it gates, how it's proved). The AIGP note explains the cross-reference rationale.
6. parallel_form_with_siblings — structure and depth of framework_mappings consistent with siblings under the same BP.

All six ≥ 2 to pass.

COMMON FAILURE MODES

- AIGP reference present but AWS missing — primary anchor convention broken.
- mapping_state: 'verified' on a reference that hasn't been checked against source.
- implementation_step.verbatim_text paraphrased rather than copied verbatim.
- note field empty — no explanation of what the principle adds.
- AWS reference uses a different BP than the principle is actually concretising.
user_template:
Author the framework_mappings for this principle.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ statement_description }}</description>
</statement>

<aws_anchor>
  <lens>{{ aws_lens }}</lens>
  <pillar>{{ aws_pillar }}</pillar>
  <focus_area>{{ aws_focus_area }}</focus_area>
  <question>{{ aws_question }}</question>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
  <implementation_step>
    <number>{{ aws_step_number }}</number>
    <title>{{ aws_step_title }}</title>
    <verbatim_text>{{ aws_step_verbatim }}</verbatim_text>
  </implementation_step>
</aws_anchor>

<siblings_framework_mappings>
{{ sibling_framework_mappings }}
</siblings_framework_mappings>

<today_iso_date>{{ today_iso_date }}</today_iso_date>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

schema.framework_mappings: object keyed by framework — each framework key holds { references: [ { common_fields, framework_specific_fields } ] }

references.question: GENOPS01 — How do you achieve and verify consistent model output quality?

implementation_step.title: Create a ground truth dataset
implementation_step.verbatim_text: Create a ground truth dataset. Verify that you have diverse data representation. Consider various user personas and use cases.

references.note: AWS step 1 calls for the dataset to exist with diverse representation. AWS leaves unspecified: where the dataset lives, who owns it, what shape it takes, and how strata are declared. This principle concretises all four.

references.domain: Domain III: Understanding How to Govern AI Development
references.competency: III.A: Govern Data for AI Systems
references.note: Provisional mapping based on subject-matter proximity. Needs a side-by-side review against the AIGP III.A competency definition to promote to verified.
